name: New-Module
-ArgumentList
-Args
options.description: Specifies an array of arguments which are parameter values that are passed to the script block. For more information about the behavior of ArgumentList , see about_Splatting (about/about_Splatting.md#splatting-with-arrays).

options.name: -AsCustomObject
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet returns a custom object that represents the dynamic module. The module members are implemented as script methods of the custom object, but they are not imported into the session. You can save the custom object in a variable and use dot notation to invoke the members.

options.name: -Cmdlet
options.description: Specifies an array of cmdlets that this cmdlet exports from the module into the current session. Enter a comma-separated list of cmdlets. Wildcard characters are permitted. By default, all cmdlets in the module are exported.

options.name: -Function
options.description: Specifies an array of functions that this cmdlet exports from the module into the current session. Enter a comma-separated list of functions. Wildcard characters are permitted. By default, all functions defined in a module are exported.

options.name: -Name
options.description: Specifies a name for the new module. You can also pipe a module name to New-Module.

options.name: -ReturnResult
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet runs the script block and returns the script block results instead of returning a module object.

options.name: -ScriptBlock
options.description: Specifies the contents of the dynamic module. Enclose the contents in braces (`{}`) to create a script block. This parameter is required.

description: Creates a new dynamic module that exists only in memory.
